quotes: At first we tried applying the trace formula to the full L-function family, but the spectral gap vanished; restricting to Dirichlet characters with conductor coprime to 37 preserved the constraint.

Connes objected that the Hilbert-Pólya operator isn't self-adjoint on the critical strip; we answered by constructing a rigged Hilbert space where the deficiency indices vanish precisely when Re(s)=1/2.
The error term improvement from O(x^(1/2) log x) to O(x^(1/2)) looks marginal, but it crosses the threshold where Cramér's conjecture becomes decidable in finite time — that's the real payoff.
